steps.error_hint: Anthropic's postgres-mcp is read-only by design. Ensure the DB user has SELECT on information_schema and pg_catalog.

steps.action: Ask GPT to diff this week's snapshot against last week's and write a plain-English summary

input_mapping.system: You are a database change reviewer. Flag new tables, dropped columns, and row-count swings >20%.

steps.error_hint: Truncate large snapshots to stay under the model's context window. Use a structured JSON diff first if tables >200.

steps.action: Post the weekly schema digest to the database-ops channel
steps.error_hint: Use Block Kit sections for long summaries — plain text over ~3000 chars may be truncated in the preview.

goal: Detect and alert on slow Postgres queries via GitLab issue creation
description: Agent inspects pg_stat_statements for queries slower than a threshold, deduplicates against recent alerts, and creates a GitLab issue assigned to the database team.

steps.action: Query pg_stat_statements for calls with mean_exec_time > 1000ms in the last hour

input_mapping.query: SELECT queryid, query, calls, mean_exec_time, total_exec_time FROM pg_stat_statements WHERE mean_exec_time > 1000 ORDER BY total_exec_time DESC LIMIT 20

steps.error_hint: pg_stat_statements must be enabled in postgresql.conf via shared_preload_libraries. Query text may be truncated to pg_stat_statements.track_activity_query_size.

steps.action: Search for existing open issues with label 'slow-query' and the same queryid to avoid duplicates
steps.error_hint: GitLab search is full-text — include the queryid as a stable token in issue bodies so dedup is reliable.

steps.action: Create new issues for slow queries that don't yet have an open tracker
steps.error_hint: Use project-scoped Project Access Tokens instead of user PATs for long-running agents — they're revocable per-project.

goal: Warm an Upstash Redis cache with hot data from PostgreSQL before peak hours
description: Before a daily traffic peak, the agent reads the most-accessed entities from PostgreSQL via the official MCP server, writes them into Upstash Redis with a TTL, and reports cache fill stats to Teams.

steps.action: Read the top-N hot rows from the source table (e.g., products by view count last 7d)

input_mapping.query: SELECT id, data FROM products ORDER BY views_7d DESC LIMIT 500

steps.error_hint: postgres-mcp is read-only by design. Aggregate views_7d via a materialized view if the OLTP table is hot.

steps.action: SET each row as a JSON value with a 6-hour TTL
steps.error_hint: Use the Upstash REST API pipeline endpoint for batch writes — single SETs will hit rate limits on large warmups.

steps.action: Post cache warmup completion to the platform-ops Teams channel
steps.error_hint: Teams Graph API requires Chat.ReadWrite or ChannelMessage.Send. Use Adaptive Cards for richer formatting.

goal: Automatically invalidate Redis cache when PostgreSQL data changes
description: Listens for PostgreSQL change events, identifies affected cache keys in Redis, invalidates them, and logs the operation.

steps.action: Detect a data change event via LISTEN/NOTIFY or logical replication
steps.error_hint: PostgreSQL NOTIFY payloads are limited to 8000 bytes — for large rows, send only the row ID and fetch details separately

steps.action: Invalidate all cache keys matching the changed entity pattern
steps.error_hint: Avoid KEYS command in production Redis — use SCAN for pattern matching to prevent blocking the server

steps.action: Log the cache invalidation event to an ops channel for audit
steps.error_hint: Only log invalidations above a threshold (e.g., >10 keys) to avoid flooding the ops channel

steps.error_hint: Use search_depth='advanced' for deeper results (costs more credits). api_key goes in POST body, not header.

steps.action: Deep-dive analysis using Tavily findings as context, with web citations
steps.error_hint: Use model='sonar-pro' for best quality with citations. API requires payment method even for first use.

steps.action: Create research page in Notion database with analysis and sources
steps.error_hint: Notion API uses block-based content — convert markdown to Notion blocks. Max 100 blocks per request.

goal: Sync Treasure Data segments to Looker for visualization
description: Export Treasure Data audience segments and make them available in Looker dashboards for business intelligence reporting.

steps.action: Run scheduled query to export audience segment data
steps.error_hint: Treasure Data query may take minutes for large datasets. Use Presto for faster interactive queries, Hive for batch

steps.action: Trigger PDT (Persistent Derived Table) rebuild to refresh segment data
steps.error_hint: Looker PDT rebuild can be slow. Use datagroups for smart caching instead of forced rebuild

steps.action: Notify analytics team that segment data has been refreshed

input_mapping.message: Segment data refreshed: {{row_count}} records updated in Looker

steps.error_hint: Include Looker dashboard link in notification for quick access

goal: Deploy Turso embedded replicas at the edge with Fly.io
description: Turso creates an embedded replica database, Fly.io deploys it to edge regions, and a health check verifies replication lag.

steps.action: Create an embedded replica database for the target edge region
steps.error_hint: Turso free tier allows only 3 databases — check quota before creating replicas

steps.action: Deploy the application with the Turso embedded replica to the specified Fly.io region
steps.error_hint: Fly.io region codes (e.g., 'nrt', 'iad') don't match Turso region names — maintain a mapping table

steps.action: Report the edge deployment status and replication health
steps.error_hint: Never post auth tokens to Slack — redact sensitive connection details from the notification

goal: kintone deal close to SMS thank-you via Twilio
description: When a deal is closed-won in kintone, send a personalized thank-you SMS to the customer via Twilio and log the activity. Japanese business courtesy automation.

steps.action: Get closed-won deal details with customer phone number
steps.error_hint: Japanese phone numbers: mobile starts with 070/080/090. Format as +81XXXXXXXXX (drop leading 0).

steps.action: Send personalized thank-you SMS

input_mapping.body: {{customer_name}}様、この度はご契約ありがとうございます。今後ともよろしくお願いいたします。

steps.error_hint: Twilio JP: use +81 format. Japanese SMS has 670 char limit (Shift_JIS). Keep under 70 chars for single segment.

steps.action: Update deal record with SMS sent timestamp
steps.error_hint: Mark as sent to prevent duplicate SMS. Use kintone's revision field for optimistic concurrency.

goal: Log Twilio call records to Salesforce activities
description: After a Twilio call ends, create a Salesforce activity record with call details, recording link, and associate with the matching contact.

steps.action: Receive call completion status callback with duration and recording URL
steps.error_hint: Twilio status callback URL must be publicly accessible. Use ngrok for development testing

steps.action: Find contact by phone number and create task/activity with call details
steps.error_hint: Japanese phone numbers may be stored with or without country code (+81). Search both formats

goal: Automated social media scheduling
description: Monitor Twitter engagement metrics and use Buffer to schedule optimized posts across platforms.

steps.action: Fetch recent tweet performance metrics to identify best-performing content
steps.error_hint: Free tier: 1500 tweets read/month. Use tweet.fields=public_metrics for engagement data. OAuth 2.0 PKCE for user context.

steps.action: Schedule posts inspired by top-performing content across connected profiles
steps.error_hint: Request body is form-encoded, NOT JSON. Profile = connected social account. List profiles first (GET /profiles.json).

goal: Track Twitter engagement events in Segment for downstream analytics
description: Twitter API captures engagement data (likes, retweets, replies), Segment normalizes and routes the events, enabling downstream analytics tools to process social metrics.

steps.action: Fetch recent engagement metrics for specified tweets or account
steps.error_hint: Twitter API v2 rate limits are strict (300 requests/15min for app auth) — batch tweet IDs in a single request

steps.action: Send normalized engagement events to Segment for routing
steps.error_hint: Segment's track call requires a userId or anonymousId — use the Twitter account ID as the userId

steps.action: Post a social engagement summary to the marketing channel
steps.error_hint: Include the time period in the Slack message — engagement rates without context are meaningless

goal: Form submission to nurture sequence
description: Typeform form completion triggers a behavioral nurture sequence in Customer.io.

steps.action: Retrieve form response with answers keyed by field ref
steps.error_hint: Responses are keyed by field.ref — set stable refs during form creation. Personal access token as Bearer.

steps.action: Create or update customer and track form_completed event
steps.error_hint: Track API uses Basic auth (site_id:api_key), NOT Bearer. Customer ID is YOUR choice — use email or internal ID.

goal: Automate new SaaS customer onboarding after payment
description: When a Stripe payment succeeds, provision a Supabase user, send a welcome email, and notify the team.

steps.action: Receive checkout.session.completed webhook with customer email and plan
steps.error_hint: Verify webhook signature to prevent spoofed events. Use Stripe CLI for local testing.

steps.action: Create user record with auth and profile data in the database
steps.error_hint: Check if email already exists to handle duplicate payment events idempotently

steps.action: Send welcome email with setup instructions and login link
steps.error_hint: Verify sending domain is authenticated in Resend dashboard. Check spam score.

steps.action: Notify the sales channel about the new customer signup
steps.error_hint: Use a dedicated #new-customers channel to avoid noise in general sales chat

goal: Answer support questions from a Qdrant-backed knowledge base
description: When a user posts a question in Slack, the agent embeds the query with OpenAI, finds the top-5 most similar passages in Qdrant, generates an answer grounded in those passages, and replies in-thread.

steps.action: Receive @mention in a support channel and extract the question text
steps.error_hint: Strip the @bot mention from the text before embedding. Use event_ts as thread_ts if thread_ts is missing.

steps.action: Embed the question with the same model used for indexing
steps.error_hint: Use the SAME embedding model as the index build — mismatched models produce meaningless similarity scores.

steps.action: Similarity search: top-5 nearest passages with payload metadata
steps.error_hint: A score_threshold filters out irrelevant results. Tune it based on your corpus — typically 0.6-0.8 for cosine similarity.

steps.action: Generate an answer grounded ONLY in the retrieved passages

input_mapping.system: Answer the user's question using ONLY the provided passages. If the answer isn't there, say 'I don't know'.

steps.error_hint: Explicitly instruct the model to refuse when the passages don't contain the answer — this prevents hallucination.

steps.action: Reply in-thread with the answer and citations
steps.error_hint: Always cite source paths so users can verify. In-thread replies prevent channel noise.

goal: Capture leads from a Webflow site via Typeform into a marketing CRM
description: A Typeform embedded in a Webflow site captures lead information, which flows into ActiveCampaign for nurture sequence enrollment.

steps.action: Receive and parse the completed Typeform submission
steps.error_hint: Typeform hidden fields must be configured to pass UTM params from the Webflow URL — they don't flow automatically

steps.action: Create or update a contact in ActiveCampaign and add to a nurture automation
steps.error_hint: ActiveCampaign custom fields must be created in the UI before the API can set them — unknown fields are silently dropped

steps.action: Notify the sales team about the new lead in real time
steps.error_hint: Avoid posting PII like phone numbers in public Slack channels — use a private channel or DM the assigned rep

goal: Archive Yayoi accounting receipts to Box
description: Export receipt images and transaction data from Yayoi and archive them in Box organized by fiscal year and month.

steps.action: Export receipts and associated transaction metadata for archival period
steps.error_hint: Yayoi API (Misoca) has separate auth from Yayoi desktop. Ensure cloud version credentials are used

steps.action: Upload receipt files to year/month folder structure with metadata
steps.error_hint: Box folder auto-creation may fail if parent doesn't exist. Create year folder first, then month

goal: Year-end tax adjustment workflow (年末調整)
description: Collect employee documents, process year-end adjustment calculations, and submit to tax office.

steps.action: Collect employee year-end adjustment documents (扶養控除申告書, 保険料控除申告書)
steps.error_hint: SmartHR is API-only. Send reminder to employees with missing documents before deadline (typically mid-November).

steps.action: Calculate year-end adjustment (withholding tax reconciliation) for each employee
steps.error_hint: Verify housing loan deduction (住宅ローン控除) amounts. First year requires tax office submission, second year onwards through employer.

steps.action: Send year-end adjustment completion report to HR team

input_mapping.message: 年末調整 {{tax_year}} complete. {{employee_count}} processed. Total refund: ¥{{total_refund}}

steps.error_hint: Include count of employees with additional tax due vs refund

goal: Aggregate YouTube channel analytics into BigQuery for weekly Slack reporting
description: YouTube API pulls video performance metrics, BigQuery stores and aggregates them, and a formatted weekly summary is posted to Slack.

steps.action: Fetch video analytics for the channel over the past 7 days
steps.error_hint: YouTube Analytics API has a 2-day data delay — set date_range_end to 2 days ago, not today

steps.action: Insert the weekly metrics into the analytics table and run the aggregation query
steps.error_hint: BigQuery streaming inserts have a brief delay before data is queryable — add a short wait before the aggregation query

steps.action: Post the weekly YouTube analytics summary to the content team channel
steps.error_hint: Format large numbers with commas for readability — raw integers in Slack messages are hard to scan

goal: Use Zapier as middleware to connect non-API services to kintone
description: Set up Zapier webhook to receive data from external sources and push to kintone app, with error logging to Google Sheets.

steps.action: Receive incoming webhook data and apply data transformation
steps.error_hint: Zapier webhook URL changes if you recreate the Zap. Pin the URL in config management

steps.action: Create or update record in target app based on source type
steps.error_hint: Zapier kintone integration uses subdomain.cybozu.com format. Include .cybozu.com in domain config

goal: Backup Slack files to Google Drive via Zapier
description: Use Zapier to monitor Slack channels for shared files and automatically archive them to a Google Drive folder.

steps.action: Monitor Slack file_shared events and trigger file download
steps.error_hint: Zapier Slack trigger for files requires files:read scope. Check Zapier Slack connection permissions

steps.action: Upload file to designated backup folder with original filename and metadata
steps.error_hint: Google Drive storage quota applies. Monitor usage and alert before hitting limit

goal: Zendesk support ticket escalation to HubSpot deal
description: When a Zendesk ticket reveals an upsell opportunity (e.g., customer asks about features on higher plan), create a deal in HubSpot and notify the account owner.

steps.action: Get ticket details with customer info and conversation history
steps.error_hint: Zendesk requester may not have organization set — fall back to email domain for company identification

steps.action: Find or create contact, then create deal in upsell pipeline
steps.error_hint: Use HubSpot search API to find existing contact by email before creating a new one (avoid duplicates)

steps.action: Notify account owner about upsell opportunity with ticket context
steps.error_hint: Tag the deal owner with @mention using their Slack user ID

goal: Mirror Zendesk tickets to kintone for cross-team visibility
description: Replicate Zendesk support tickets into a kintone app so non-support teams can track customer issues without Zendesk licenses.

steps.action: Capture new ticket creation and status updates via webhook
steps.error_hint: Zendesk triggers fire on specific conditions. Create trigger for 'ticket is created' and 'ticket is updated'

steps.action: Create or update ticket mirror record with current status and tags
steps.error_hint: Use kintone's update-by-query with Zendesk ticket ID as key to handle create vs update logic

goal: Escalate high-priority Zendesk tickets to Slack
description: When a Zendesk ticket is marked urgent or has been unresolved beyond SLA, escalate to the engineering Slack channel.

steps.action: Detect ticket priority change to urgent or SLA breach trigger
steps.error_hint: Zendesk webhook triggers require Admin role. Test with Zendesk webhook tester tool

steps.action: Post escalation alert with ticket details and direct link to Zendesk
steps.error_hint: Use Slack Block Kit for rich formatting. Include assignee mention using Slack user ID mapping

goal: Customer support escalation to engineering bug tracking
description: Zendesk ticket tagged as bug automatically creates Linear issue and notifies engineering via Slack.

steps.action: Detect ticket tagged with 'bug' or priority escalation
steps.error_hint: Zendesk trigger must fire on tag addition or priority change

steps.action: Create bug issue in engineering team with ticket context
steps.error_hint: Linear API key must have issue create permission

steps.action: Alert engineering channel with bug details and Linear link
steps.error_hint: Bot must be in engineering channel
